race.name: Triple Bypass
race.tagline: 3 mountain passes. 118 miles. Nearly 11,000 feet of climbing across the Continental Divide. Colorado's defining cycling challenge since 1988.

vitals.registration: Online via triplebypass.org. Sells out. Cost: ~$265
vitals.feed_zones: 4 fully stocked aid stations including lunch at Loveland Ski Area. SAG support entire route.
vitals.cutoff_time: Aid station cutoffs enforced. Finish line closes at 8:00 PM.
route_options: Triple Bypass: 118 miles, 10,800 ft — 3 passes (Juniper, Loveland, Vail)
Double Bypass: 80 miles, 6,500 ft — 2 passes (Loveland, Vail), starts in Empire

climate.primary: Colorado Rocky Mountain summer — variable, altitude-dependent
climate.description: Mid-July in the Colorado Rockies means warm starts in Evergreen (50-65F at dawn), cold pass summits (40-55F at 11,000+ ft), and the ever-present threat of afternoon thunderstorms above treeline. Temperature drops approximately 3.5F per 1,000 feet of elevation gain. The early start is strategic — you want to be off Loveland Pass before the afternoon lightning arrives.
challenges: Temperature range from cool dawn to warm valleys to cold pass summits
Afternoon thunderstorms — lightning above treeline is life-threatening
High-altitude sun exposure — intense UV above 9,000 ft
Thin air throughout — entire ride above 7,200 ft, passes approaching 12,000 ft
Rapidly changing conditions at altitude — sun to hail in minutes

terrain.primary: Paved mountain highways and dedicated bike paths
terrain.surface: Paved mountain roads and paved bike paths along I-70 corridor. Roads closed to traffic on key segments on event day.
features: Juniper Pass (11,140 ft) — first climb from Evergreen, rewarded with fast descent into Idaho Springs
Loveland Pass (11,990 ft) — the crux climb with switchbacks to nearly 12,000 ft, crossing the Continental Divide
Vail Pass (10,660 ft) — the final pass, primarily on dedicated bike path
Swan Mountain — the unofficial 'fourth pass' between Keystone and Frisco
28-mile descent from Vail Pass to Avon on the I-70 bike path to finish

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: A three-act mountain epic. The ride builds from forested foothills through the alpine tundra of the Continental Divide and into the heart of Colorado ski country. Each pass has its own personality — Juniper is the opener, Loveland is the crux, Vail is the closer. The final 28-mile descent from Vail Pass to Avon on the dedicated bike path is one of the great finishing stretches in American cycling. You earn every foot of that descent.
course_description.signature_challenge: Loveland Pass — the switchbacks above treeline to 11,990 ft, crossing the Continental Divide. The thin air, the exposed alpine tundra, the endless turns — this is the moment that defines the Triple Bypass and the image that stays with every rider who completes it.
suffering_zones: The Victory Descent — 28 Miles to Avon

logistics.airport: Denver International (DEN) — 1 hour to Evergreen. Straightforward I-70 West access.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Evergreen has very limited lodging — the one hotel sells out fast. Golden (20 min east) has multiple hotel options and is the recommended pre-ride base. For the finish, Avon and Vail have abundant (but expensive) hotels — SpringHill Suites and TownePlace Suites in Avon are official event sponsors. Book early: July is peak season in Summit County and Vail Valley.
logistics.food: Aid stations provide real food including lunch at Loveland Ski Area. Finish line in Avon has beer and food. Evergreen has restaurants for pre-ride dinner. Avon and Vail have extensive post-ride dining.

history.origin_story: In 1988, a group of avid cyclists from Team Evergreen decided it would be fun and challenging to ride from Evergreen to the Vail Valley, crossing three mountain passes in a single day. What started as an informal sufferfest among friends grew into Colorado's signature cycling event. Team Evergreen — Colorado's largest cycling club — ran the event for decades before reorganizing into Shift Events on the production side and Evergreen Ride Club on the social side. Thirty-seven years and over $4.6 million in charitable donations later, the Triple Bypass is more than a ride — it's a Colorado institution.
notable_moments: 1988: Inaugural ride — a group of Team Evergreen cyclists ride Evergreen to the Vail Valley
Grew from informal challenge to Colorado's largest single-day cycling event
Over $4.6 million donated to community nonprofits including Special Olympics, American Diabetes Association, and Bicycle Colorado
Team Evergreen split into Shift Events (event production) and Evergreen Ride Club (2020s)
Field grew from a handful of friends to 5,000-rider sellout — annually one of the first events to fill
history.reputation: Colorado's cycling rite of passage. The three-pass challenge that every Front Range road cyclist puts on their list.

biased_opinion.summary: The Triple Bypass is Colorado's cycling identity made manifest. Three mountain passes. Nearly 12,000 feet at the summit of Loveland. The Continental Divide crossed on two wheels. Since 1988, this ride has been the measuring stick for Front Range cyclists — the annual question: can I get over all three? It's not a race, and it doesn't pretend to be. It's a 118-mile conversation between you and the Rocky Mountains, starting in the dark foothill pines of Evergreen and ending with a 28-mile victory lap descent into the Vail Valley. Over $4.6 million donated to community nonprofits. 37 years without interruption. This is what cycling for something looks like.
strengths: Three iconic passes including Loveland at 11,990 ft — crossing the Continental Divide on a bike
Spectacular Rocky Mountain scenery from foothills to alpine tundra to ski country to Vail Valley
37 years of tradition with $4.6M donated — one of US cycling's great community institutions
The 28-mile finishing descent from Vail Pass to Avon is one of the best reward descents in American cycling
Denver airport proximity: unlike most mountain events, getting here is straightforward
weaknesses: Not competitive: no timing, no awards. If you need racing, this is not your event.
Altitude: the entire ride is above 7,200 ft. Sea-level riders will suffer more than expected.
Afternoon thunderstorms: lightning above treeline at 12,000 ft is genuinely dangerous. Start early.
Peak tourist season pricing: Summit County and Vail Valley hotels in July are expensive
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you own a road bike and have ever looked at the Rocky Mountains and thought 'I want to ride over those,' the Triple Bypass is the ride. Three passes. One day. Thirty-seven years of Colorado cyclists will tell you the same thing: it changes you.

final_verdict.one_liner: 3 passes. 11,990 feet at the summit. Colorado's defining cycling challenge since 1988.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want to cross the Continental Divide on a bicycle and experience the full spectrum of Colorado mountain riding in a single day. Yes if sustained high-altitude climbing on paved roads is your definition of a good time. Skip if you need race competition or can't tolerate riding above 10,000 feet.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want more climbing with Sierra Nevada scenery: try Death Ride. If you want European mountain pass prestige: try La Marmotte. If you want a multi-day Colorado cycling adventure: try Ride the Rockies. If you want US climbing with a competitive edge: try Levi's GranFondo.

videos.title: Triple Bypass | Course Preview: Buchanan Park - Juniper Pass (version 5)
videos.description:
This preview depicts the first climb of Triple Bypass (visit https://triplebypass.org to learn more), an epic bike ride through Colorado's Rocky Mountains.

Starting in Evergreen and finishing in Avon, riders will cover 120 miles and ascend 11,000 feet as they cross three mountain passes Juniper Pass, Loveland Pass, and Vail Pass.

Set to "Tour de France" from the Chasing Legends Original Motion Picture Soundtrack, used with generous permission from Haik Naltchayan. Visit https://www.imdb.com/name/nm2298416/ to learn more, or follow these links to listen to the track:

- https://music.apple.com/us/album/le-tour-de-france/394260620?i=394260683
- https://open.spotify.com/track/1n9Q6xeDQli7wVL48oyK96?si=2e7c182f586749f4
videos.curation_reason: Recent 2023 course preview showing specific terrain details of the first climb from Buchanan Park to Juniper Pass.

rider_intel.search_text: The Triple Bypass is one of Colorado's premier cycling events, running from Evergreen to Avon and covering three mountain passes with 10,000 feet of climbing. Founded 28 years ago by Team Evergreen, the ride started when 20 people showed up for a club ride that eventually grew into this major challenge. The event draws 5,000 cyclists from across the country and internationally. Riders describe the route as having super fast ascents and unreal scenery through the most beautiful parts of Colorado. The event offers both single and double options, with registration available at triplebypass.org. Fire danger has occasionally forced cancellations, with alternative rides offered around Evergreen at Buchanan Park. The ride is considered a significant personal accomplishment and challenge that attracts riders who return annually to improve their performance.
